reasoning: The document suffers from significant structural failures, most notably the duplication of the entire resolution body text within a high-contrast block and the fragmentation of the signature section. Although programmatic word counts appear stable, the visual score of 0.25 and the specific findings regarding missing names (Brian Fitzpatrick) and broken image elements for signatures present a high risk for a formal government resolution.

concerns.analysis: The visual organization of the HTML output is significantly different from the source PDF. In the original document, the county seal is positioned at the top center as a header element. In the HTML output, it has been moved to the middle of the document, following the main resolution text. Furthermore, the signature block at the bottom has been dismantled; the names are placed into an empty metadata table, while the handwritten signatures are rendered as fragmented images further down the page.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF shows a unified vertical flow: Seal/Header -> Title -> Resolution Text -> Signatures at the bottom.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows the seal moved to the middle, signatures separated into an empty table and fragmented images, and the entire content rearranged into a high-contrast block layout at the bottom.

concerns.analysis: Meaningful images from the source, specifically the handwritten signatures, are rendered as broken or fragmented elements. The signature for Taylor McCurry is truncated, missing the leading 'T' and appearing as 'aylor McCurry'. Furthermore, in the high-contrast section at the bottom, the signatures and header graphics are fragmented into individual boxes and fragments (e.g., a standalone 'T' and 'M'), which fails to preserve the meaningful visual integrity of the original signatures.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Handwritten signatures for 'Taylor McCurry' and 'Steve Pitman' on the bottom of page 1.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML screenshot shows a signature image truncated to 'aylor McCurry' and additional fragmented signature components in the bottom section.
concerns.location: Signature area below the resolution text and within the high-contrast section at the bottom.

concerns.analysis: The entire body content of the resolution is visually duplicated within the rendered HTML. The resolution text, from the title through to the adoption date, appears once in a standard layout at the top of the page and is repeated in its entirety within the high-contrast block section at the bottom of the page. This is a direct duplication of genuine body content.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The text 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING THE RECLASSIFICATION OF MITCHELL COUNTY 911 DISPATCHERS... Adopted this the 21st day of February, 2023.' appears exactly once.
concerns.output_quote: The resolution text starting with 'RESOLUTION SUPPORTING...' appears at the top of the output and is repeated starting near the middle of the screenshot on a black background.
concerns.location: The entire main body content of the document.

concerns.description: Minor text differences beyond acceptable reformatting
concerns.analysis: There is a minor text difference in the first 'WHEREAS' clause. The source text identifies the sponsors as 'US Reps Norma T. Torres and Brian Fitzpatrick', while the output omits the word 'Reps', identifying them only as 'US Norma T. Torres'.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: WHEREAS, in September of 2021, US Reps Norma T. Torres and Brian Fitzpatrick announced...
concerns.output_quote: WHEREAS, in September of 2021, US Norma T. Torres and Brian Fitzpatrick announced...

action_items: Delete the duplicated content block at the end of the document
Repair the signature block to ensure all names and signatures are correctly displayed and legible
Restore the missing text 'Reps' and 'Brian Fitzpatrick' in the first clause
Relocate the county seal to the top center as per the source PDF
